text.title: Design-to-Dev Handoff — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that survive AI-generated Figma frames and prevent the most common implementation drift: tokens, states, motion, content rules, focus order.

rule.statement: Every color, spacing, radius, typography style, and elevation in the handoff MUST be specified as a design-token reference (e.g., color/text/primary, space/4), NOT a raw value (#1F2937, 16px). Frames that contain raw values fail the gate.

rationale.source: W3C Design Tokens spec, Material Design / Polaris / Carbon token systems

rule.rationale: Raw values destroy theming, dark mode, and brand updates. They also indicate the designer copy-pasted from an AI export without re-binding to the system. Dev who implements raw values creates an immediate tech-debt entry.

rule.statement: Every interactive element MUST have all required states rendered or explicitly marked N/A with reason. Required by element type: button (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading), input (default, hover, focus, filled, invalid, disabled, loading), card-action (default, hover, focus, pressed), navigation-item (default, hover, focus, active, current-page).

rationale.source: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices, design-systems state inventories

rule.rationale: Missing states are the #1 dev-handoff defect. AI-Figma typically renders only "default" and "hover", silently dropping focus and disabled. Devs then invent states inconsistently across screens.

rule.statement: Every transition or animation in the design MUST declare: duration (ms), easing (named curve from design system: ease-out, ease-in-out, spring(stiffness, damping)), trigger (hover / focus / state-change / on-mount / scroll), and prefers-reduced-motion fallback. "Smooth" / "subtle" / "snappy" without numbers fail the gate.

rationale.source: Material Motion / Apple HIG motion guidelines, WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions

rule.rationale: Without numbers, dev guesses. Guesses don't compose into a system. prefers-reduced-motion is a WCAG AAA requirement and an accessibility lawsuit risk if ignored.

rule.statement: Every text element MUST declare: max length OR character budget, truncation strategy (ellipsis / wrap-N-lines / scroll), i18n expansion factor (e.g., German +35%), and empty-state copy if applicable. Lorem ipsum or "real copy" without rules fails the gate.

rationale.source: Material text design, i18n-engineering literature, Smashing Magazine i18n studies

rule.rationale: Text that fits in English overflows in German / Russian and breaks layout. Without truncation rules, dev picks one and it's wrong. i18n expansion factor lets dev test the worst case before ship.

rule.statement: Every screen MUST include a focus-order annotation (numbered overlay or dev-mode annotation): the sequence in which Tab moves through interactive elements. Keyboard traps (modal scope, custom widgets) MUST be called out with the trap boundary.

rationale.source: WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order, WAI-ARIA modal patterns

rule.rationale: Focus order is invisible in static Figma frames. Without annotation, dev implements DOM order, which often diverges from visual order and breaks keyboard navigation. Lawsuit-grade accessibility defect.

example.title: Button spec with tokens, states, motion, content rule
example.code:
Element: PrimaryButton
Tokens: bg: color/action/primary/default (hover: /hover, disabled: /disabled) text: color/text/on-primary padding: space/y/3 + space/x/4 radius: radius/md
States: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading loading: spinner uses motion/spin (1.2s linear infinite)
Motion: bg color transition: 150ms ease-out (token: motion/standard) prefers-reduced-motion: skip transition, snap to new color
Content: max length: 22 chars truncation: none (button width adjusts; if > 22, use SecondaryActionMenu instead) i18n expansion: 35% (max 30 chars in DE/RU)
Focus order: receives focus on Tab after the preceding input; visible focus ring uses color/focus/ring at 2px.
example.note: Illustrates r1-r5 all in one element spec.

example.title: AI-Figma frame with no spec
example.code:
Element: PrimaryButton - background: #4F46E5 - color: white - padding: 12px 16px - "smooth hover effect"
States rendered: default, hover (no focus, no disabled, no loading) Content: "Submit" No motion duration, no i18n note, no focus order.
Dev outcome: - Used inline hex (no theme), invented focus ring color, invented disabled style, omitted loading, picked 300ms ease-in-out for hover. - Three buttons across the app have three different focus rings.
Repair: re-do with r1-r5 applied. Or use the design system component; do not author from raw frame.
example.note: Illustrates r1-r3 + r5 violations — the standard AI-Figma drop.
